title: The Structure and Interpretation of Computer Programs
description: Introduction to programming and computer science using Python, with an emphasis on abstraction, recursion, and programming paradigms.

title: The United States from Civil War to Present
description: Survey of modern United States history with attention to politics, culture, and social movements.

title: Ancient Philosophy
description: Introduction to the major figures and problems of ancient Greek philosophy, including Plato and Aristotle.

title: Introduction to General Astronomy
description: Non-technical survey of modern astronomy, from planets and stars to galaxies and cosmology.

title: Introduction to Cognitive Science
description: Interdisciplinary overview of how minds and brains work, drawing on psychology, computer science, philosophy, and linguistics.

title: Principles of Business
description: Introductory survey of business disciplines including finance, marketing, operations, and organizational behavior.

title: Public Speaking and the Art of Persuasion
description: Workshop-focused course on effective public speaking, argumentation, and storytelling.

title: Foundations of Data Science
description: Introduction to data science through data manipulation, visualization, and inference using real-world datasets.

title: Introduction to Political Theory
description: Survey of classic works in political theory from ancient to modern authors, with an emphasis on concepts of justice and power.

title: Multivariable Calculus
description: Vector calculus in two and three dimensions, including partial derivatives and multiple integrals.

title: Foundations of Legal Studies
description: Interdisciplinary introduction to law in social context, including legal institutions, processes, and theory.

title: Introduction to Visual Thinking
description: Studio-based course focused on the fundamentals of visual composition, drawing, and creative experimentation.

title: History of Information
description: Explores how societies have created, stored, and used information from ancient archives to the digital age.

title: Ethics in Science and Engineering
description: Case-based exploration of ethical questions in modern science and engineering practice.

title: Introduction to Media Studies
description: Introduction to key concepts and debates in media and communication, from television to social media.
